title: Puccinellia paupercula
intro: Puccinellia paupercula, also known as slender alkali grass, is a perennial plant species native to the Arctic tundra and subarctic regions of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues and protect against cellular damage. The bioactive constituents of Puccinellia paupercula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Puccinellia paupercula is often prepared as a tea or infused in a tincture, and it is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create respiratory and immune system support formulations.
taxonomy: Puccinellia paupercula belongs to the family Poaceae. This family is commonly known as the grass family. Puccinellia paupercula is also classified under the subfamily Pooideae and the tribe Poeae. The genus Puccinellia is a group of grasses that are typically found in wetlands or grasslands. They are native to the Northern Hemisphere and have a global distribution.
